title: The Prayer that Jesus Refused to Pray
summary: “Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels? But how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be?”Mat 26:53-54 When the Lord told the apostles to sell that they had and buy swords before he went to Jerusalem to be arrested and sentenced to death, the apostles misunderstood and procured two swords. “And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, It is enough” (Luke 22:38). Galileans were known to be straightforward people who did not shy away from a confrontation, and they interpreted the words of the Lord literally. Once they came to Jerusalem, the Lord was arrested. They were 13 including the Lord. How can two swords between 13 men fight against the well-armed soldiers of the high priest who were also backed up by the Roman soldiers? Yet because Peter’s love for the Lord was so great, he ignored the odds against them and struck the high priest, Malchus’ head with his sword. Fortunately, Peter missed the head and cut off an ear.
